item.title: 【Java Microservices(SCS) vs. Spring Modulith】Java 微服务 (SCS) 与 Spring Modulith
item.description:
【

This article discusses the differences between a Java microservice architecture (SCS style) using Clean Architecture and a Spring Modulith architecture. It explores their strengths, trade-offs, and when to use each approach.

The architectures are demonstrated using two projects:

】

本文讨论使用 Clean Architecture 的 Java 微服务架构（SCS 风格）与 Spring Modulith 架构之间的差异。它探讨了它们的优势、权衡以及何时使用每种方法。

使用两个项目演示了这些架构：

item.title: 【Zero-Cost AI with Java】使用 Java 实现零成本 AI
item.description:
【

So you have a new AI-based idea and need to create an MVP app to test it?

If your AI knowledge is limited to OpenAI, I have bad news for you… it’s not going to be free.

】

那么您有一个基于 AI 的新想法，需要创建一个 MVP 应用来测试它？

如果您的 AI 知识仅限于 OpenAI，我有坏消息告诉您......它不会是免费的。

item.title: 【How Piezoelectric Energy Harvesting Is Solving the Battery Waste Crisis in Industrial IoT】压电能量收集如何解决工业物联网中的电池浪费危机
item.description:
【

High-temperature energy harvesting exposes the hidden cost of batteries across Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) deployments, especially in environments where heat and access constraints shorten battery life and raise maintenance risk. Fit-and-forget architectures matter in hazardous and remote locations. Battery replacement introduces downtime and unpredictable operating costs that scale with fleet size, while thermal extremes further reduce cell reliability.

Energy harvesting and self-powered sensors emerge as engineering-driven solutions that align with long-term system availability and life-cycle performance. Battery-less IIoT designs become a practical response to operational constraints rather than a sustainability narrative.

】

高温能量收集暴露了工业物联网 (IIoT) 部署中电池的隐性成本，尤其是在热量和访问限制缩短电池寿命并增加维护风险的环境中。一劳永逸的架构在危险和偏远地区非常重要。电池更换会导致停机时间和不可预测的运营成本，这些成本会随着车队规模的扩大而增加，而极端温度会进一步降低电池的可靠性。

能量收集和自供电传感器作为工程驱动的解决方案出现，与长期系统可用性和生命周期性能相一致。无电池工业物联网设计成为对运营限制的实际反应，而不是可持续发展的叙述。

item.title: 【How LLMs Reach 1 Million Token Context Windows — Context Parallelism and Ring Attention】LLM 如何达到 100 万个令牌上下文窗口 - 上下文并行性和环注意力
item.description:
【

Context Length and Hardware Scalability

Context windows have exploded from 4k tokens to 10 million in just a few years. Meta's Llama 4 Scout supports 10M tokens — 78x more than Llama 3's 128k. Google's Gemini 3 Pro handles 1M tokens, while Claude 4 offers 1M in beta.

This enables processing entire codebases, hundreds of research papers, or multi-day conversation histories in a single pass. But there's a problem: context length has outpaced hardware capacity.

】

上下文长度和硬件可扩展性

上下文窗口在短短几年内就从 4k 个代币激增至 1000 万个。 Meta 的 Llama 4 Scout 支持 10M 代币 — 比 Llama 3 的 128k 多 78 倍。 Google 的 Gemini 3 Pro 可处理 100 万个代币，而 Claude 4 在测试版中可处理 100 万个代币。

这使得能够一次处理整个代码库、数百篇研究论文或多天的对话历史记录。但有一个问题：上下文长度超过了硬件容量。

item.title: 【Is Your “Human-in-the-Loop” Actually Slowing You Down? Here’s What We Learned】你的“人机循环”真的会减慢你的速度吗？这是我们学到的东西
item.description:
【

In the rush to adopt AI and automation, many teams implement human-in-the-loop (HITL) frameworks. They believe that involving a person in the process solves the problems with reliability, quality, and trust. But as we’ve learned from real engineering workflows and integrations, the story isn’t that easy. In some contexts, humans-in-the-loop do improve outcomes, but in others, they can unintentionally become bottlenecks that limit speed, scalability, and innovation.

In this post, we’ll analyze when human-in-the-loop is truly valuable, when it slows systems down, and how to strike the right balance between automation and human judgment.

What Does “Human-in-the-Loop” Really Mean?

Human-in-the-loop refers to the integration of human judgment into automated decision workflows, particularly in machine learning and AI systems. Instead of allowing algorithms to run fully autonomously, systems are designed so humans intervene at key points to approve, reject, correct, or guide outputs. This pattern includes:

】

在急于采用人工智能和自动化的过程中，许多团队实施了人机交互 (HITL) 框架。他们相信，让一个人参与这个过程可以解决可靠性、质量和信任方面的问题。但正如我们从真实的工程工作流程和集成中了解到的那样，事情并不那么容易。在某些情况下，人机交互确实可以改善结果，但在其他情况下，它们可能会无意中成为限制速度、可扩展性和创新的瓶颈。

在这篇文章中，我们将分析人机交互何时真正有价值、何时减慢系统速度，以及如何在自动化和人类判断之间取得适当的平衡。

“人在环”的真正含义是什么？

人机循环是指将人类判断集成到自动化决策工作流程中，特别是在机器学习和人工智能系统中。系统的设计不是让算法完全自主运行，而是让人类在关键点进行干预以批准、拒绝、纠正或指导输出。该模式包括：

item.title: 【Fast Data Access Part 2: From Manual Hacks to Modern Stacks】快速数据访问第 2 部分：从手动黑客到现代堆栈
item.description:
【

It's been a while since I wrote Part 1 of this series. If you recall, back in 2019, we built a "Fast Data" pipeline using GemFire 9 and Spark 2.4.

Precap of Part 1: Do you remember the pain we went through?

】

自从我撰写本系列的第 1 部分以来，已经有一段时间了。如果您还记得，早在 2019 年，我们就使用 GemFire 9 和 Spark 2.4 构建了“快速数据”管道。

第 1 部分前言：您还记得我们经历过的痛苦吗？

item.title: 【Essential Monitoring Metrics for Cloud Native Systems: Part 1】云原生系统的基本监控指标：第 1 部分
item.description:
【

Monitoring Is Not a Dashboard-Only Problem

In the last couple of years, I have moved across a few product teams. Every time I walk into an engineering team and ask how monitoring works. I get a standard response.

| There is a dashboard

】

监控不仅仅是仪表板的问题

在过去的几年里，我换过几个产品团队。每次我走进工程团队并询问监控是如何工作的。我得到了标准回复。

| 有一个仪表板

item.title: 【Orchestrating the Agentic Explosion: A Unified Governance Framework for the AI-First Enterprise】精心策划代理爆炸：人工智能优先企业的统一治理框架
item.description:
【

The Dawn of Agentic Chaos

In 2026, the enterprise landscape has shifted from AI as a tool to AI as a Digital Teammate. Recent industry studies from IDC and Deloitte indicate that by the end of this year, nearly one-third of all AI-enabled applications will rely on autonomous agents. Technology companies in 2026 envision anchored democratized agent creation, allowing any role from a financial market analyst to a senior architect to deploy a functional digital assistant in minutes.

However, this democratization has given rise to a new organizational crisis: agent sprawl. Without a centralized orchestration strategy, enterprises face redundant compute costs, double agent security risks, and a fragmented logic layer that threatens the integrity of the corporate data estate. For AI Architect and Strategy leaders, the challenge is no longer just delivery; it is creating a unified agent governance framework that balances the speed of "citizen development" with the rigors of production-grade stability.

】

代理混沌的黎明

2026 年，企业格局已从 AI 作为工具转变为 AI 作为数字团队伙伴。 IDC 和德勤最近的行业研究表明，到今年年底，近三分之一的人工智能应用程序将依赖于自主代理。到 2026 年，科技公司设想创建锚定的民主化代理，允许从金融市场分析师到高级架构师的任何角色在几分钟内部署功能性数字助理。

然而，这种民主化引发了新的组织危机：代理蔓延。如果没有集中编排策略，企业将面临冗余的计算成本、双重代理安全风险以及威胁企业数据完整性的支离破碎的逻辑层。对于人工智能架构师和战略领导者来说，挑战不再只是交付，而是交付。它正在创建一个统一的代理治理框架，以平衡“公民发展”的速度与生产级稳定性的严格性。

item.title: 【Beyond the Black Box: Implementing “Human-in-the-Loop” (HITL) Agentic Workflows for Regulated Industries】超越黑匣子：为受监管行业实施“人在环”(HITL) 代理工作流程
item.description:
【

The Technical Hook

Autonomous agents exhibit failure patterns analogous to those in distributed systems: not through isolated catastrophic errors, but via a cascade of locally justifiable actions that collectively result in globally unsafe states. Prompt injection in AI systems parallels a forged remote procedure call (RPC) syntactically valid input that traverses multiple processing layers before inducing an unauthorized state transition. 

As illustrated in Figure 1, this architectural risk is mitigated by the "Commit Boundary," which prevents adversarial inputs from reaching sensitive executors by validating every intent against a deterministic schema. When extended with capabilities such as tool invocation and long-term planning, these agents manifest failure modes like confused deputy scenarios and privilege escalation, which are neutralized by the layered enforcement framework depicted in the diagram.

】

技术挂钩

自治代理表现出类似于分布式系统中的故障模式：不是通过孤立的灾难性错误，而是通过一系列局部合理的操作，这些操作共同导致全局不安全状态。人工智能系统中的即时注入与伪造的远程过程调用（RPC）语法上有效的输入并行，该输入在引发未经授权的状态转换之前遍历多个处理层。 

如图 1 所示，这种架构风险通过“提交边界”得到缓解，“提交边界”通过根据确定性模式验证每个意图来防止对抗性输入到达敏感执行器。当通过工具调用和长期规划等功能进行扩展时，这些代理会表现出故障模式，例如混乱的代理场景和权限升级，这些故障模式可以通过图中所示的分层执行框架来抵消。

item.title: 【The Invisible Bleed: A Field Guide to Cloud Costs That Hide in Plain Sight】看不见的流血：隐藏在众目睽睽之下的云成本现场指南
item.description:
【

You deploy on Friday. The pipeline goes green. Monday morning, finance forwards you a bill that's double what it should be, and nobody can explain why. This scenario repeats across thousands of engineering teams — not because they're careless, but because cloud infrastructure has a peculiar talent for concealing its own inefficiencies.

I've spent the better part of a decade debugging systems that worked perfectly yet hemorrhaged money. The patterns are weirdly consistent. What follows isn't theory — it's the accumulated scar tissue from watching well-architected systems quietly bankrupt themselves.

】

您在周五部署。管道变绿。周一早上，财务部门给你寄来了一份比应有金额双倍的账单，但没有人能解释原因。这种情况在数千个工程团队中不断重复 - 不是因为他们粗心，而是因为云基础设施具有隐藏自身低效率的特殊才能。

十年来的大部分时间我都在调试运行完美但却损失惨重的系统。这些图案出奇地一致。接下来的不是理论——而是看着架构良好的系统悄然破产而积累的疤痕组织。
